Package org.aavso.tools.vstar.util.date

Class J2000HJDConverter

java.lang.Object
org.aavso.tools.vstar.util.date.AbstractHJDConverter
org.aavso.tools.vstar.util.date.J2000HJDConverter
public class J2000HJDConverter extends AbstractHJDConverter

This class contains a method for converting a Julian Date to a Heliocentric Julian Date using the Low Accuracy method in Jean Meeus's Astronomical Algorithms, ch 24.

See also:
- Meeus, ch 21, 24
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heliocentric_Julian_Day - http://britastro.org/computing/applets_dt.html
- http://www.physics.sfasu.edu/astro/javascript/hjd.html

Values are computed as degrees and converted to radians when necessary (when passing to a trigonometric function). Where a variable name is not qualified with a "Degs" suffix, it can be assumed to be in radians or to be a non-trigonometric value.

Variable names often follow Meeus's conventions since this allows easier correspondence between Meeus and code. This sometimes means abandoning the usual Java naming conventions, e.g. using a single letter capital variable name. Comments show the correspondence between code and equations in Meeus.

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    double
    convert(double jd, RAInfo ra, DecInfo dec)
    Given a JD and a target's RA and Dec, return the Heliocentric Julian Date.
    protected double
    degsInRange(double n)
    Given a value in degrees, return the corresponding value in the range 0 to 360.
    See, for example, http://www.purplemath.com/modules/radians2.htm
    protected double
    dmsToDegs(int degs, int mins, double secs)
    Given degrees, minutes, and seconds, return decimal degrees.
    protected double
    eccentricity(double T)
    Given the time in Julian centuries, return eccentricity of Earth's orbit.
    protected double
    hjd(double jd, double T, SolarCoords coords, RAInfo ra, DecInfo dec)
    Compute and return the Heliocentric JD of the target.
    protected double
    julianCenturies(double jd)
    Time measured in Julian centuries of 36525 ephemeris days from epoch J2000.0 (2000 January 21.5 TD).
    TODO: do we need to doing anything else for this to be in JDE/TD?
    protected double
    longitudeOfAscendingNode(double T)
    Return the longitude of ascending node of Moon's mean orbit on ecliptic, measured from mean equinox of date.
    protected double
    meanObliquityHighPrecision(double T)
    Given the time in Julian Centuries from epoch J2000.0, return the mean obliquity of the ecliptic in radians.
    protected double
    meanObliquityLowPrecision(double T)
    Given the time in Julian Centuries from epoch J2000.0, return the mean obliquity of the ecliptic in radians.
    protected double
    obliquity(double T)
    Given the time in Julian Centuries from epoch J2000.0, return the obliquity of the ecliptic in radians.
    protected double
    radiusVector(double T, double M, double C)
    Given the time in Julian centuries, return radius vector (Earth-Sun distance) in AU.
    protected double
    radsInRange(double n)
    Given a value in radians, return the corresponding value in the range 0 to 2PI.
    See, for example, http://www.purplemath.com/modules/radians2.htm
    protected double
    secsToRads(double secs)
    Given a value in seconds, return the corresponding number of radians.
    protected SolarCoords
    solarCoords(double T, int year)
    Given the year and time in Julian centuries, return the Sun's coordinates.

  • Constructor Details

    • J2000HJDConverter

      public J2000HJDConverter()

  • Method Details

    • convert

      public double convert(double jd, RAInfo ra, DecInfo dec)
      Given a JD and a target's RA and Dec, return the Heliocentric Julian Date.
      Specified by:
      convert in class AbstractHJDConverter
      Parameters:
      jd - The Julian Date to be converted.
      ra - The J2000 epoch right ascension coordinate in decimal degrees.
      dec - The J2000 epoch declination coordinate in decimal degrees.
      Returns:
      The corresponding Heliocentric Julian Date.

    • julianCenturies

      protected double julianCenturies(double jd)
      Time measured in Julian centuries of 36525 ephemeris days from epoch J2000.0 (2000 January 21.5 TD).
      TODO: do we need to doing anything else for this to be in JDE/TD?
      Parameters:
      jd - The Julian Date.
      Returns:
      The time in Julian centuries.

    • hjd

      protected double hjd(double jd, double T, SolarCoords coords, RAInfo ra, DecInfo dec)
      Compute and return the Heliocentric JD of the target.
      Parameters:
      jd - The Julian Date to be converted.
      T - The time in Julian centuries.
      coords - The Solar coordinates for the julian date.
      ra - The J2000 epoch right ascension coordinate in decimal degrees.
      dec - The J2000 epoch declination coordinate in decimal degrees.
      Returns:
      The corresponding Heliocentric Julian Date.

    • solarCoords

      protected SolarCoords solarCoords(double T, int year)
      Given the year and time in Julian centuries, return the Sun's coordinates.
      Parameters:
      T - The time in Julian centuries.
      year - The year.
      Returns:
      The solar coordinates for the specified date.

    • longitudeOfAscendingNode

      protected double longitudeOfAscendingNode(double T)
      Return the longitude of ascending node of Moon's mean orbit on ecliptic, measured from mean equinox of date.
      Parameters:
      T - The time in Julian centuries.
      Returns:
      The longitude of the ascending node of Moon's mean orbit in radians.

    • radiusVector

      protected double radiusVector(double T, double M, double C)
      Given the time in Julian centuries, return radius vector (Earth-Sun distance) in AU.
      Parameters:
      T - The time in Julian centuries.
      M - The true solar anomaly.
      C - The Sun's equation of center.
      Returns:
      The Sun's radius vector in AU.

    • eccentricity

      protected double eccentricity(double T)
      Given the time in Julian centuries, return eccentricity of Earth's orbit.
      Parameters:
      T - The time in Julian centuries.
      Returns:
      The eccentricity in radians.

    • obliquity

      protected double obliquity(double T)
      Given the time in Julian Centuries from epoch J2000.0, return the obliquity of the ecliptic in radians.
      Parameters:
      T - Time measured in Julian centuries from epoch J2000.0.
      Returns:
      The obliquity in radians.

    • meanObliquityLowPrecision

      protected double meanObliquityLowPrecision(double T)
      Given the time in Julian Centuries from epoch J2000.0, return the mean obliquity of the ecliptic in radians.
      Parameters:
      T - Time measured in Julian centuries from epoch J2000.0.
      Returns:
      The mean obliquity in radians.

    • meanObliquityHighPrecision

      protected double meanObliquityHighPrecision(double T)
      Given the time in Julian Centuries from epoch J2000.0, return the mean obliquity of the ecliptic in radians.
      Parameters:
      T - Time measured in Julian centuries from epoch J2000.0.
      Returns:
      The mean obliquity in radians.

    • secsToRads

      protected double secsToRads(double secs)
      Given a value in seconds, return the corresponding number of radians.
      Parameters:
      secs - The value in seconds.
      Returns:
      The corresponding value in radians.

    • dmsToDegs

      protected double dmsToDegs(int degs, int mins, double secs)
      Given degrees, minutes, and seconds, return decimal degrees.
      Parameters:
      degs - integer degrees
      mins - integer minutes
      secs - double seconds
      Returns:
      The corresponding value in decimal degrees.

    • radsInRange

      protected double radsInRange(double n)
      Given a value in radians, return the corresponding value in the range 0 to 2PI.
      See, for example, http://www.purplemath.com/modules/radians2.htm
      Parameters:
      n - The value in radians.
      Returns:
      The corresponding value in the range 0 to 2PI.

    • degsInRange

      protected double degsInRange(double n)
      Given a value in degrees, return the corresponding value in the range 0 to 360.
      See, for example, http://www.purplemath.com/modules/radians2.htm
      Parameters:
      n - The value in degrees.
      Returns:
      The corresponding value in the range 0 to 360.
